title: Unexcepted behavior of range in left-hand side expression
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

RTL code :
```
module top(data, sel, out);
input data;
input [1:0] sel;
output reg [3:1] out;

always @ (*) begin
  out = 0;
  out[sel] = data;
end
endmodule
```
yosys script synth.ys :
```
# read design
read -sv test.v
hierarchy -top top

# the high-level stuff
proc; fsm; opt; memory; opt

# mapping to internal cell library
techmap; opt

# mapping flip-flops to mycells.lib
dfflibmap -liberty ./examples/cmos/cmos_cells.lib

# mapping logic to mycells.lib
abc -liberty ./examples/cmos/cmos_cells.lib

# cleanup
clean

write_verilog -noexpr synth.v
```
The problem is reproducible with:
`$ yosys synth.ys`

## Actual behavior
synth.v :
```
/* Generated by Yosys 0.9+4309 (git sha1 9432400e, gcc 9.3.0-17ubuntu1~20.04 -fPIC -Os) */

(* hdlname = "\\top" *)
(* top =  1  *)
(* src = "test.v:1.1-10.10" *)
module top(data, sel, out);
  wire _0_;
  (* src = "test.v:2.7-2.11" *)
  input data;
  (* src = "test.v:4.18-4.21" *)
  output [3:1] out;
  (* src = "test.v:3.13-3.16" *)
  input [1:0] sel;
  NAND _1_ (
    .A(sel[0]),
    .B(data),
    .Y(_0_)
  );
  NOR _2_ (
    .A(sel[1]),
    .B(_0_),
    .Y(out[1])
  );
  assign out[3:2] = 2'h0;
endmodule
```
As shown in the verilog, output out[3:2] is assigned to zero, which is not as expected. 
And I believe the error is caused by the code that "replace dynamic ranges in left-hand side expressions" in simplify.cc .
code_blocks:
# read design
read -sv test.v
hierarchy -top top

# the high-level stuff
proc; fsm; opt; memory; opt

# mapping to internal cell library
techmap; opt

# mapping flip-flops to mycells.lib
dfflibmap -liberty ./examples/cmos/cmos_cells.lib

# mapping logic to mycells.lib
abc -liberty ./examples/cmos/cmos_cells.lib

# cleanup
clean

write_verilog -noexpr synth.v
